mdx:
# SimpleGrid

SimpleGrid offers an intuitive way to easily create responsive grid layouts.

## Basic usage

Use SimpleGrid → When you need a quick, responsive, equal-sized grid (e.g.,
product listings, image galleries).

```jsx
import { SimpleGrid } from "@commercetools/nimbus";
```

```jsx-live
const App = () => (
  <SimpleGrid gap="100">
    <Box key="1" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box key="2" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 2
    </Box>
  </SimpleGrid>
)
```

## With columns

Use the columns prop to define the number of columns in the grid layout.

```jsx-live
const App = () => (
  <SimpleGrid gap="100" columns={2}>
    <Box key="1" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box key="2" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 2
    </Box>
    <Box key="3" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 3
    </Box>
    <Box key="4" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 4
    </Box>
  </SimpleGrid>
)
```

## Auto responsive layout

An Auto-Responsive Grid dynamically adjusts the number of columns and item sizes
based on the viewport width, ensuring an adaptive and fluid layout. adjust
viewport to see layout rearrangements.

```jsx-live
const App = () => (
  <SimpleGrid gap="100" minChildWidth="sm">
    <Box key="1" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box key="2" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 2
    </Box>
    <Box key="3" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 3
    </Box>
    <Box key="4" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 4
    </Box>
  </SimpleGrid>
)
```

## Simple grid with colSpan

```jsx-live
const App = () => (
  <SimpleGrid
    columns={{ base: 2, md: 4 }}
    gap={{ base: "600", md: "1000" }}
  >
    <SimpleGrid.Item colSpan={{ base: 1, md: 3 }}>
      <Box height="20" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Column 1
      </Box>
    </SimpleGrid.Item>
    <SimpleGrid.Item colSpan={{ base: 1, md: 1 }}>
      <Box height="20" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Column 2
      </Box>
    </SimpleGrid.Item>
  </SimpleGrid>
)
```

## With row and column gap

```jsx-live
const App = () => (
  <SimpleGrid rowGap="400" columnGap="200" columns={2}>
    <Box key="1" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box key="2" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 2
    </Box>
    <Box key="3" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 3
    </Box>
    <Box key="4" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 4
    </Box>
  </SimpleGrid>
)
```

<PropsTable id="SimpleGrid" />

## Basic usage

dev.mdx:
## Getting started ### Import ```tsx import { SimpleGrid, type SimpleGridProps } from '@commercetools/nimbus'; ``` ### Basic usage SimpleGrid creates a grid layout with equal-sized children. Use it for quick, responsive layouts without complex grid configuration: ```jsx-live-dev const App = () => ( Item 1 Item 2 Item 3 Item 4 ) ``` ## Usage examples ### Fixed columns Use the `columns` prop to define a fixed number of columns: ```jsx-live-dev const App = () => ( Two columns Item 1 Item 2 Item 3 Item 4 Three columns Item 1 Item 2 Item 3 Item 4 Item 5 Item 6 ) ``` ### Auto-responsive layout Use `minChildWidth` to create auto-responsive grids that adjust column count based on available space: ```jsx-live-dev const App = () => ( Card 1 Resize the window to see columns adjust Card 2 Columns wrap automatically Card 3 Based on minChildWidth Card 4 No media queries needed Card 5 Responsive by default Card 6 Equal-sized children Try resizing the browser window to see the grid adapt ) ``` ### Responsive columns Define different column counts for different breakpoints: ```jsx-live-dev const App = () => ( Product 1 $29.99 Product 2 $39.99 Product 3 $49.99 Product 4 $59.99 Product 5 $69.99 Product 6 $79.99 1 column on mobile → 2 on small → 3 on medium → 4 on large screens ) ``` ### Column spanning Use `SimpleGrid.Item` with `colSpan` to make items span multiple columns: ```jsx-live-dev const App = () => ( Featured Item Spans 2 columns on mobile, 3 on desktop Sidebar Full width on mobile Item 1 Item 2 Item 3 Item 4 ) ``` ### Custom gap spacing Control row and column gaps independently: ```jsx-live-dev const App = () => ( Equal gap on all sides Item 1 Item 2 Item 3 Item 4 Item 5 Item 6 Different row and column gaps Item 1 Item 2 Item 3 Item 4 Item 5 Item 6 ) ``` ### Responsive gap values Gap spacing can also be responsive: ```jsx-live-dev const App = () => ( Item 1 Gap increases on larger screens Item 2 Item 3 Item 4 Item 5 Item 6 ) ``` ## Component requirements ### Usage context SimpleGrid should be used when you need: - Quick, responsive grid layouts with minimal configuration - Equal-sized grid items (product cards, image galleries, data cards) - Auto-responsive layouts that adapt to container width - Simple column-based layouts without complex positioning **When to use SimpleGrid vs Grid:** Use **SimpleGrid** when: - All items should be equal size - You need auto-responsive behavior with `minChildWidth` - Layout is based on a simple column count - You want minimal configuration Use **Grid** when: - Items need different sizes (row/column spanning) - You need grid template areas for complex layouts - Precise control over grid structure is required - Creating dashboard layouts or complex page structures ## Accessibility SimpleGrid is a layout component that doesn't add semantic meaning by itself. Ensure accessible markup: #### Semantic Structure - Wrap related content in appropriate semantic elements (`
`, `
`, `
`) - Use proper heading hierarchy within grid items - Ensure grid items have meaningful content structure ```tsx

Available Products

{products.map(product => (

{product.name}

{product.description}

))} ``` #### Keyboard navigation - Grid layout doesn't affect keyboard navigation - Ensure interactive elements within grid items are keyboard accessible - Maintain logical tab order #### Persistent ID If your use case requires tracking and analytics for this component, it is good practice to add a **persistent**, **unique** id to the component: ```tsx const PERSISTENT_ID = "product-grid"; export const ProductGrid = () => ( Product 1 Product 2 Product 3 ); ``` ## API reference ## Common patterns ### Product grid Display products in a responsive grid layout: ```jsx-live-dev const App = () => { const products = [ { id: 1, name: "Wireless Mouse", price: "$29.99", stock: "In Stock" }, { id: 2, name: "Mechanical Keyboard", price: "$89.99", stock: "In Stock" }, { id: 3, name: "USB-C Hub", price: "$49.99", stock: "Low Stock" }, { id: 4, name: "Monitor Stand", price: "$39.99", stock: "In Stock" }, { id: 5, name: "Laptop Sleeve", price: "$24.99", stock: "In Stock" }, { id: 6, name: "Cable Organizer", price: "$14.99", stock: "In Stock" }, ]; return ( {products.map((product) => ( {product.name} {product.price} {product.stock} ))} ); } ``` ### Image gallery Create responsive image galleries: ```jsx-live-dev const App = () => { const images = [ { id: 1, alt: "Landscape 1", color: "primary.7" }, { id: 2, alt: "Landscape 2", color: "info.7" }, { id: 3, alt: "Landscape 3", color: "positive.7" }, { id: 4, alt: "Landscape 4", color: "warning.7" }, { id: 5, alt: "Landscape 5", color: "critical.7" }, { id: 6, alt: "Landscape 6", color: "neutral.7" }, ]; return ( {images.map((image) => ( {image.alt} ))} ); } ``` ### Dashboard widgets Layout dashboard cards in a responsive grid: ```jsx-live-dev const App = () => { const stats = [ { label: "Total Sales", value: "$12,345", change: "+12.5%", trend: "up" }, { label: "Orders", value: "1,234", change: "+8.2%", trend: "up" }, { label: "Customers", value: "5,678", change: "-2.4%", trend: "down" }, { label: "Revenue", value: "$45,678", change: "+15.3%", trend: "up" }, ]; return ( {stats.map((stat, index) => ( {stat.label} {stat.value} {stat.change} ))} ); } ``` ### Feature cards Display feature highlights in a grid: ```jsx-live-dev const App = () => { const features = [ { icon: Icons.Speed, title: "Fast Performance", description: "Lightning-fast load times and smooth interactions" }, { icon: Icons.Security, title: "Secure by Default", description: "Enterprise-grade security for your data" }, { icon: Icons.CloudDone, title: "Cloud Storage", description: "Unlimited cloud storage for all your files" }, { icon: Icons.Devices, title: "Cross-Platform", description: "Works seamlessly across all your devices" }, { icon: Icons.Support, title: "24/7 Support", description: "Round-the-clock customer support" }, { icon: Icons.Analytics, title: "Analytics", description: "Detailed insights and reporting tools" }, ]; return ( {features.map((feature, index) => ( {feature.title} {feature.description} ))} ); } ``` ## Testing your implementation These examples demonstrate how to test your implementation when using SimpleGrid within your application. As the component's internal functionality is already tested by Nimbus, these patterns help you verify your integration and application-specific logic. ### Basic Rendering Tests Verify SimpleGrid renders with expected structure and children ```tsx import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import { SimpleGrid, Box, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("SimpleGrid - Basic rendering", () => { it("renders children in a grid layout", () => { render( Item 1 Item 2 Item 3 ); const grid = screen.getByTestId("grid"); expect(grid).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByTestId("item-1")).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByTestId("item-2")).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByTestId("item-3")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); it("renders with correct display style", () => { render( Item ); const grid = screen.getByTestId("grid"); expect(grid).toHaveStyle({ display: "grid" }); }); it("forwards data attributes", () => { render( Item ); const grid = screen.getByTestId("test-grid"); expect(grid).toHaveAttribute("data-custom", "value"); }); }); ``` ### Column Configuration Tests Test different column configurations and responsive behavior ```tsx import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import { SimpleGrid, Box, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("SimpleGrid - Column configuration", () => { it("applies fixed column count", () => { render( Item 1 Item 2 Item 3 ); const grid = screen.getByTestId("grid"); expect(grid).toHaveStyle({ gridTemplateColumns: "repeat(3, minmax(0, 1fr))", }); }); it("applies minChildWidth for auto-responsive layout", () => { render( Item 1 Item 2 ); const grid = screen.getByTestId("grid"); expect(grid).toHaveStyle({ gridTemplateColumns: "repeat(auto-fit, minmax(200px, 1fr))", }); }); it("supports responsive column values", () => { render( Item 1 Item 2 Item 3 ); const grid = screen.getByTestId("grid"); expect(grid).toBeInTheDocument(); // Responsive values are applied via CSS classes expect(grid).toHaveClass(/css-/); }); }); ``` ### Gap Spacing Tests Test gap, columnGap, and rowGap properties ```tsx import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import { SimpleGrid, Box, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("SimpleGrid - Gap spacing", () => { it("applies uniform gap", () => { render( Item 1 Item 2 ); const grid = screen.getByTestId("grid"); expect(grid).toBeInTheDocument(); // Gap is applied via Chakra tokens }); it("applies separate column and row gaps", () => { render( Item 1 Item 2 ); const grid = screen.getByTestId("grid"); expect(grid).toBeInTheDocument(); }); it("supports responsive gap values", () => { render( Item 1 Item 2 ); const grid = screen.getByTestId("grid"); expect(grid).toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); ``` ### Column Spanning Tests Test SimpleGrid.Item with colSpan property ```tsx import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import { SimpleGrid, Box, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("SimpleGrid - Column spanning", () => { it("renders SimpleGrid.Item with colSpan", () => { render( Spanning Item Item 1 Item 2 ); const spanItem = screen.getByTestId("span-item"); expect(spanItem).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByText("Spanning Item")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); it("supports responsive colSpan values", () => { render( Responsive Spanning Item Item 1 ); const responsiveSpan = screen.getByTestId("responsive-span"); expect(responsiveSpan).toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); ``` ### Accessibility Tests Verify accessibility attributes and semantic structure ```tsx import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import { SimpleGrid, Box, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("SimpleGrid - Accessibility", () => { it("forwards aria-label attribute", () => { render( Item 1 ); const grid = screen.getByTestId("grid"); expect(grid).toHaveAttribute("aria-label", "Product grid"); }); it("supports custom element types with as prop", () => { render( Item 1 ); const grid = screen.getByTestId("grid"); expect(grid.tagName).toBe("SECTION"); }); it("maintains accessible structure with semantic children", () => { render(

Products

Product 1

Product 2

 ); const grid = screen.getByTestId("grid"); expect(grid).toHaveAttribute("aria-labelledby", "grid-heading"); expect(screen.getByText("Product 1")).toBeInTheDocument(); expect(screen.getByText("Product 2")).toBeInTheDocument(); }); }); ``` ### Ref Forwarding Tests Verify ref forwarding to the underlying DOM element ```tsx import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest"; import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react"; import { SimpleGrid, Box, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus"; describe("SimpleGrid - Ref forwarding", () => { it("forwards ref to the grid element", () => { const ref = { current: null as HTMLDivElement | null }; render( Item 1 ); expect(ref.current).toBeInstanceOf(HTMLDivElement); expect(ref.current).toHaveAttribute("data-testid", "grid"); }); it("allows access to DOM methods through ref", () => { const ref = { current: null as HTMLDivElement | null }; render( Item 1 ); expect(ref.current?.querySelector).toBeDefined(); expect(ref.current?.getBoundingClientRect).toBeDefined(); }); }); ``` ## Resources - [Storybook](https://nimbus-storybook.vercel.app/?path=/docs/components-simplegrid--docs) - [Chakra UI SimpleGrid Documentation](https://www.chakra-ui.com/docs/components/simple-grid)
